weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week. Hypertrophy Week 2 — two quick re-tests (Bench and Slams) to set your block baseline, then the same movements as last week with a little more. We're training the box squat with dumbbells this block so you can add small 5–10 lb jumps — comp day is still the kettlebell. The sled is still down: the Prowler card has substitutes this week and real sled pushes return next week. Stay controlled on the eccentric; stop a set when form breaks.
testsLineHtml: Test week. Two baselines for the new block — Bench Press for max reps at your competition weight, and Med Ball Slams for max slams in 60 seconds. Warm up, give a real effort, then tap your name below to log each one.

patterns.name: Tempo Box Squat
patterns.rx: 4 × 12–15 reps · rest 90 sec between · 3-sec descent + 1-sec pause on the box
patterns.load: A dumbbell held at your chest (goblet), or two DBs racked at your shoulders if a single bell gets awkward — your number above is your starting load; nudge it up 5 lb when all 4 sets are clean
cues: This is your competition event — we're training it with dumbbells this block so you can add small 5 lb jumps. Comp day is still the kettlebell.
Lower over a slow 3-count, sit fully on the box for one second, then stand.
Keep the weight tight to your chest (or stacked on your shoulders), elbows in, heels flat on the floor.
The last 2–3 reps should be hard but clean — stop the set if your chest dumps forward.

patterns.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 10–12 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand — add about 5 lb per hand over last week if 12 was clean
cues: Back foot up on the Bulgarian split-squat stand, front foot a full stride forward.
Drop straight down — front knee tracks over the toes, back knee toward the floor.
Drive up through the front heel so the working leg does the work, not the back foot.
Only add weight if you held your balance for all reps last week.

patterns.name: Heels-Elevated Goblet Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 18 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: One dumbbell held at your chest — same weight as last week
cues: Heels up on a small plate or wedge, chest tall.
Sit straight down between your knees to full depth, no bounce at the bottom.
Keep the extra reps controlled — three good reps beat three rushed ones.
Hold the dumbbell tight to your chest the whole set.

patterns.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
patterns.rx: 4 × 10–12 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand (or a loaded barbell) — a touch heavier than last week if your back stayed flat
cues: Push your hips back and slide the weights down your thighs — this is a hinge, not a squat.
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings, then drive your hips forward to stand tall.
Keep your back flat and the weights close to your legs the whole way.
Add load only if last week's reps stayed smooth top to bottom.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · rest 60 sec between sets · 9 crisp throws total
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men), competition weight
cues: Load your hips back, then explode tall and throw up and back over your head in one motion.
Reset fully between every throw — this is sharp technique and distance, not getting tired.
Finish each throw on your toes with your arms reaching high.

patterns.name: DB Pullover
patterns.rx: 3 × 12–15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: One dumbbell held in both hands over the chest, lying on a bench — same or slightly heavier than last week
cues: Lie on the bench and hold one dumbbell over your chest with both hands.
Lower it back over your head only as far as your shoulders allow with no pain.
Pull it back over your chest using your lats, keeping a soft bend in the elbows.

patterns.name: Bench Press · Max Reps
patterns.rx: 1 × max reps at your competition bench weight · all-out to clean technical failure
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight (shown above) — use a spotter or safety arms
cues: Warm up thoroughly, then do one all-out set: as many clean reps as you can at your comp weight.
Lower to the chest under control and press up — stop the set when a rep gets ugly or a spotter has to help.
This number resets your block baseline and sets your volume targets for the rest of Hypertrophy.

patterns.name: Incline DB Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12–15 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Dumbbells slightly heavier than last week if you finished all 3 sets in range
cues: Press the dumbbells up and slightly together over your upper chest.
Lower under control until you feel a stretch, then drive back up.
Do this after your bench test — don't pre-fatigue the test.

patterns.name: Close-Grip Push-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × sub-max reps · rest 90 sec between · leave 2–3 in the tank
patterns.load: Bodyweight — hands narrow under your shoulders (knees down or hands on a bench if needed)
cues: Hands narrow under your shoulders, elbows tucked toward your ribs.
Lower your chest under control and press all the way back up.
Drop to your knees or raise your hands onto a bench before your form falls apart.

patterns.name: Active Dead Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min between · beat last week by ~5 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight — light band-assist over the bar if needed
cues: Hang with your shoulders pulled down and back, not loose and shrugged up.
Squeeze the bar hard and breathe steadily; aim to add about 5 seconds over last week.
Drop down the moment your grip starts to slip — don't let your shoulders go slack.

patterns.name: Inverted Row
patterns.rx: 4 × 10–12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight under a bar or rings — set the bar a notch lower than last week to make it harder
cues: Keep a straight line from shoulders to heels — squeeze your glutes.
Pull your chest to the bar, leading with your elbows, not your chin.
Lower the bar a notch only if last week's reps were easy at the top.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between · add ~5 sec over last week
patterns.load: Bodyweight, gripping fat-grip attachments clamped on the bar
cues: Wrap your hands fully around the fat grips and squeeze hard.
Keep your shoulders packed down, the same as the active hang.
This is our grip-overload work — fat grips, never a towel.

patterns.name: Comp Slam Test · 60s
patterns.rx: 1 × max slams in 60 seconds · all-out
patterns.load: D-ball at your competition weight
cues: Set a timer for 60 seconds and get as many full slams as you can.
Full overhead reach every rep — pick the ball up fast and go again.
Pace the first 20 seconds so you don't blow up; this number is your block baseline.
D-ball only — never a Dynamax ball for slams.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 4 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · controlled, full reach
patterns.load: D-ball at your competition weight
cues: Reach the ball all the way overhead, then slam it down hard in front of you.
Pick it up and reset your stance before the next rep — no half-slams.
Do this after the 60-second test, not before.

patterns.name: D-Ball Clean to Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: D-ball at or one size above competition weight
cues: Hinge, grip the ball, and clean it to your chest in one move.
Bring it overhead and slam it straight down with full extension.
Keep your back flat on every clean.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec · rest 90 sec between · 85% effort
patterns.load: Jump rope sized so the handles reach your armpits when you stand on the middle
cues: Stay on the balls of your feet with small, quick hops.
Keep the turns coming from your wrists, not big arm swings.
If you trip, reset fast and keep the 60 seconds going.

patterns.name: Seated Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 4 × 15–20 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell or plate resting on your knees, heavier than last week, balls of the feet on a step
cues: Let your heels drop below the step for a full stretch, then press up onto your toes.
Pause for a beat at the top of every rep.
Add weight only if you reached 20 reps comfortably last week.

patterns.name: Standing Calf Raise (double-leg)
patterns.rx: 4 × 15–20 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand or a weight vest — heavier than last week
cues: Rise all the way up onto your toes, then lower under control to a full stretch.
Keep your knees straight so the calves do the work.
Hold a wall or rack for balance with the heavier load if needed.

patterns.name: Broad Jump
patterns.rx: 4 × 3 jumps · rest 60 sec between sets · strong but not max
patterns.load: Bodyweight, with room to land and stick
cues: Swing your arms back, load your hips, then explode forward and out.
Land soft on both feet and stick it — absorb the landing with bent knees.
Reset fully between jumps; this is about power, not getting tired.

patterns.name: Hip Thrust
patterns.rx: 4 × 12–15 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A barbell across the hips (use a pad) or a heavy dumbbell on the lap — heavier than last week if it was easy
cues: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top.
Lift until you're flat from knees to shoulders, ribs down.
Lower under control; don't bounce the weight off the floor.

patterns.name: DB Step-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand, box around knee height
cues: Plant your whole foot on the box and drive up through that heel.
Stand tall at the top, then lower under control — don't push off the bottom foot.
Keep the box at knee height; add reps before box height.

patterns.name: 500m Intervals
patterns.rx: 4 × 500m · 2:1 rest (rest twice your row time) · steady-hard
patterns.load: Concept 2 rower, damper 5–7
cues: Drive with your legs first, then swing back, then pull with your arms.
Hold a steady-hard pace you can repeat across all four — don't sprint the first one.
Rest twice as long as each row takes before the next.

patterns.name: Single-Arm DB Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each arm · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A dumbbell heavier than last week, still letting you control all 12
cues: Brace a hand and knee on a bench, back flat and parallel to the floor.
Pull the dumbbell to your hip, leading with the elbow, and squeeze your back.
Lower it all the way down for a full stretch each rep.

patterns.name: 2500m Steady
patterns.rx: 1 × 2500m continuous · 6–8 sec/500m slower than race pace
patterns.load: Concept 2 rower
cues: Settle into a relaxed, repeatable rhythm you could hold a conversation at.
Keep your stroke long and smooth — this builds the engine, it's not a race.
Sip water and keep moving; the goal is steady time on the rower.

patterns.name: 50 Yd Shuttle × 5
patterns.rx: 5 × 50-yard sprints · walk back to start between reps · 90% effort
patterns.load: Two lines 25 yards apart
cues: Sprint to the far line, plant your foot, and drive back hard.
Stay low through each turn — short choppy steps to change direction.
Walk back to fully recover between reps so each sprint stays fast.

patterns.name: 200m Repeats
patterns.rx: 5 × 200m · rest 90 sec between reps · strong, even pace
patterns.load: Track or open grass
cues: Run each 200 at a strong, even pace — not an all-out sprint.
Relax your shoulders and arms; let your legs set the rhythm.
Keep all five repeats within a few seconds of each other.

patterns.name: 5-10-5 Pro Agility
patterns.rx: 6 reps · full recovery between · max effort each
patterns.load: Three cones 5 yards apart in a line
cues: Start in the middle, sprint 5 yards one way, touch the line, then 10 the other way.
Drop your hips and stay low on every change of direction.
Take full recovery between reps so every one is max effort.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: Sled is down this week — do the two substitutes below. Real sled pushes return next week.
patterns.load: Competition load — 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Sled's out of commission this week — the DB Walking Lunge and Farmer's Carry below cover the same leg drive.
When it's back next week: body at ~45°, arms locked, push through the trunk.
Long steps with full hip extension — don't bend your elbows to absorb.

patterns.name: DB Walking Lunge
patterns.rx: 4 × 50 ft (25 ft down the turf and back) · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand — heavy, but every step controlled
cues: Lunge 25 ft down the turf, turn around, and lunge 25 ft back — that's one set.
Take a long step and drop straight down, front knee over the toes; drive up through the front heel.
Pick a weight you can carry the full 50 ft without your posture breaking.

patterns.name: Farmer's Carry
patterns.rx: 4 × 50 m · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: The heaviest dumbbells you can carry with a tall posture
cues: Stand tall, brace your trunk, and take quick, steady steps.
Squeeze the handles hard; set the DBs down before your posture breaks.
This trains the same grip, trunk, and leg drive as the sled push.
